preview_text:
The model favors Brazil at 64% to advance, while Scotland hold an 12% path to victory. The remaining probability splits toward a draw at 25%, suggesting a competitive fixture despite the heavy lean toward the favorite.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a match where Brazil enters as clear favorites, but draws occur frequently enough in tournament football that a single-elimination outcome remains uncertain. Scotland's underdog status is pronounced, yet the 25% draw probability indicates the model sees realistic scenarios where neither side breaks through in regulation.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model, with Brazil at 65% and Scotland at 14%—gaps under two percentage points. However, Polymarket prices all three outcomes equally at 33%, a stark divergence suggesting either illiquidity, speculative positioning, or different assumptions about tournament structure. The dashboard makes no claim about which source proves more accurate.
